The settlement pattern of residential blocks adjacent to industrial/garage zones is common across the Russian Federation.

The landscape, architecture, and road infrastructure are virtually identical to Russia due to shared Soviet-era urban planning. The flat terrain and temperate vegetation are consistent with the Ukrainian steppe or forest-steppe regions.

The architecture and road layout are consistent with northern Kazakhstan, though the vegetation is slightly more lush than the typical arid steppe, suggesting a more northern or river-adjacent location.
